page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, allocating exactly 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and passmark CPU physics tests. However, the system excels in specific multi-threaded workloads, achieving a strong rating in bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Memory performance is also strong, with a high PassMark memory score, though raw memory bandwidth for large block reads and writes remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c8ae.8xlarge offers a dense compute profile with dedicated cores, making it highly suitable for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs keeps resource overhead focused on raw compute power. It is well-suited for workloads such as high-performance web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, cryptography-heavy applications, and general compute-intensive tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ae.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T34 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption capabilities. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, including cryptography, multi-threaded processing, and web serving.
